item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship not start of pandemic, UN health agency says
item.description: Ther World Health Organization says it is not in the same situation as with Covid-19 because hantavirus spreads differently.

item.title: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
item.description: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

item.title: Morrisons supermarket in Wales fined £750k for dirty bakery
item.description: A judge said the case was not about a few rogue employees but showed serious and systemic failure.

item.title: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
item.description: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

item.title: Portugal and Italy will not suspend digital border checks for Brits
item.description: Reports had suggested the countries were set to follow the example of Greece, which effectively suspended biometric checks for UK nationals.

item.title: Robot wars - what an operation in Ukraine tells us about the battlefield of the near future
item.description: After Ukraine's President Zelensky said territory had been captured using just robots and drones, what is the future of unmanned warfare?

item.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
item.description: Some passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus have already taken flights to numerous countries.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship on way to Canary Islands after three evacuated
item.description: A British man is among three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms while aboard the MV Hondius.

item.title: Cruise passengers tell of life on board stranded ship after hantavirus outbreak
item.description: While passengers onboard the MV Hondius say the situation is calm, they face days at sea as officials warn the disease may have spread.

item.title: Shell latest oil giant to see profits surge due to Iran war impact
item.description: The energy giant's profits jump by nearly a quarter as it benefits from the recent volatility in the oil price

item.title: What is hantavirus and how does it spread?
item.description: The Andes strain of the virus, which can rarely be passed from person to person, has been confirmed in some passengers.

item.title: Former OpenAI board member says Elon Musk offered her sperm donations
item.description: Shivon Zilis is the mother of four of Musk's children. That relationship began as she advised OpenAI.

item.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
item.description: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

item.title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
item.description: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

item.title: Meta brings High Court challenge over Ofcom fees
item.description: Meta said Ofcom's calculations were "disproportionate"; the regulator said it would defend its position.

item.title: The no-nonsense judge calling the shots in Musk v Altman trial
item.description: The feud has fuelled a costly showdown between two tech titans.

item.title: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
item.description: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

item.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
item.description: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

item.title: Major rail disruption expected in southern England until end of day
item.description: A radio fault has been resolved but passengers could continue to face long delays, warns National Rail.

item.title: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
item.description: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

item.title: Trump's 'irresponsible war' to blame for economic slowdown, German minister says
item.description: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil blamed the US president's "irresponsible war in Iran" for harming the Germany economy.

item.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
item.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

item.title: Hantavirus strain that spreads between humans found in cruise ship passengers
item.description: South Africa's health ministry said the Andes strain of the virus was found in two confirmed cases linked to the outbreak.

item.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
item.description: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

item.title: How sunburn inspired a new way to store energy
item.description: Molecules that can capture heat could be a useful technology to decarbonise heating.
